key_insight: N-RSAV speeds up RSAV optimization by injecting randomized low-rank Hessian curvature while keeping the method’s energy-dissipation stability.
engineering_relevance: This makes RSAV-style optimization more practical for ill-conditioned large-scale problems, including physics-informed neural networks and other PDE-constrained models.
method: Approximate the Hessian with a randomized low-rank Nyström operator
Truncate negative eigenvalues to enforce positive semidefiniteness and preserve dissipation
Reuse the approximate Hessian adaptively when original and modified energies stay close
evidence_signal: The paper reports numerical tests on ill-conditioned low-rank problems, including convex quadratic cases and PINN training, where N-RSAV converges substantially faster than conventional RSAV methods.
watch_item: The approach depends on the quality of the low-rank Hessian approximation and on tuning the adaptive reuse criterion, which may be sensitive on problems without clear low-rank structure.
paper_title: Accelerating SAV-based optimization via randomized low-rank Hessian approximation
